title: The Three Little Pigs
author: Traditional
description: A classic fairy tale about three pigs who build houses of different materials.

vocabularies.meaning: dry yellow stalks of grain, used for building
thin pieces of wood from trees
hard blocks made of clay, used for building
a wild animal like a large dog
to push air out of your mouth
to breathe in and out noisily
a long time ago (used to start stories)

scenes.title: Introduction
scenes.narrator: Once upon a time, there were three little pigs. They were brothers. They lived with their mother.

scenes.title: Time to Leave
scenes.narrator: One day, the mother pig said:

dialogue.speaker: Mother
dialogue.line: You are big now. It is time to build your own houses.

scenes.title: Building Houses
scenes.narrator: The three pigs went to the forest. Each pig wanted to build a house.

dialogue.speaker: Pig 1
dialogue.line: I am lazy. I will build my house with straw. It is fast.

dialogue.speaker: Pig 2
dialogue.line: I will build my house with sticks. It is easy.

dialogue.speaker: Pig 3
dialogue.line: I will build my house with bricks. It is hard work, but it is strong.

scenes.title: The Houses Are Done
scenes.narrator: Pig 1 finished his house very fast. Pig 2 finished his house too. But Pig 3 worked all day.

dialogue.speaker: Pig 1
dialogue.line: Ha! My house is done! Now I can play.

dialogue.speaker: Pig 3
dialogue.line: I am still building. Bricks are heavy.

dialogue.speaker: Pig 1
dialogue.line: You work too hard! Come and play with me.

scenes.title: The Big Bad Wolf
scenes.narrator: The next day, a big bad wolf came to the forest. He was very hungry.

dialogue.speaker: Wolf
dialogue.line: I am hungry. I want to eat some pigs!

scenes.title: The Straw House
scenes.narrator: The wolf came to the straw house. Pig 1 was inside.

dialogue.speaker: Wolf
dialogue.line: Little pig, little pig, let me come in!

dialogue.speaker: Pig 1
dialogue.line: No! Not by the hair on my chin!

dialogue.speaker: Wolf
dialogue.line: Then I will huff, and I will puff, and I will blow your house in!

scenes.title: The Straw House Falls
scenes.narrator: The wolf blew very hard. The straw house fell down!

dialogue.speaker: Pig 1
dialogue.line: Help! Help! My house is gone!

scenes.title: Running to the Stick House
scenes.narrator: Pig 1 ran to the stick house. The wolf followed him.

dialogue.speaker: Pig 1
dialogue.line: Brother! Brother! Let me in!

dialogue.speaker: Pig 2
dialogue.line: Quick! Come inside!

dialogue.speaker: Wolf
dialogue.line: Little pigs, little pigs, let me come in!

dialogue.speaker: Pig 2
dialogue.line: No! Not by the hair on our chins!

dialogue.speaker: Wolf
dialogue.line: Then I will huff, and I will puff, and I will blow your house in!

scenes.title: The Stick House Falls
scenes.narrator: The wolf blew very hard. The stick house fell down too!

dialogue.speaker: Pig 2
dialogue.line: Oh no! Our house is gone too!

scenes.title: Running to the Brick House
scenes.narrator: The two pigs ran to the brick house. The wolf followed them.

dialogue.speaker: Pig 1
dialogue.line: Brother! Brother! Let us in!

dialogue.speaker: Pig 3
dialogue.line: Quick! Come inside!

dialogue.speaker: Wolf
dialogue.line: Little pigs, little pigs, let me come in!

dialogue.speaker: Pig 3
dialogue.line: No! Not by the hair on our chins!

dialogue.speaker: Wolf
dialogue.line: Then I will huff, and I will puff, and I will blow your house in!

scenes.title: The Brick House Stands
scenes.narrator: The wolf blew very hard. He blew and blew. But the brick house did not fall!

dialogue.speaker: Wolf
dialogue.line: Why will this house not fall?

dialogue.speaker: Pig 3
dialogue.line: Bricks are strong! You cannot blow it down!

scenes.title: The Wolf's Plan
scenes.narrator: The wolf was angry. He had another plan. He climbed on the roof.

dialogue.speaker: Wolf
dialogue.line: I will go down the chimney!

scenes.title: The End of the Wolf
scenes.narrator: Pig 3 saw the wolf on the roof. He made a fire.

dialogue.speaker: Pig 3
dialogue.line: I have an idea! Let us make a fire.

dialogue.speaker: Pig 1
dialogue.line: Good idea!

scenes.title: The Wolf Runs Away
scenes.narrator: The wolf fell down the chimney. He saw the fire. He was very scared.

dialogue.speaker: Wolf
dialogue.line: Ahhh! It is hot!

scenes.title: Happily Ever After
scenes.narrator: The wolf ran away. He never came back. The three pigs lived happily ever after. Pig 1 and Pig 2 learned an important lesson.

dialogue.speaker: Pig 3
dialogue.line: Hard work is important. Now you understand.

dialogue.speaker: Pig 1
dialogue.line: You are right. I will work hard from now on.
